auditor.summary: The build has critical issues that prevent it from satisfying the acceptance criteria. The most significant problem is that the build creates a new table `accessDeniedAuditNew` instead of modifying the existing `accessDeniedAudit` table as specified. This means the endpoint, migration, and schema are all inconsistent with each other and with the build spec. Additionally, several prior findings remain unresolved, including the UserRole enum reuse, the REVOKE statement using a placeholder role, and the strict datetime validation. The build needs substantial rework to converge.

top_issues.description: The build creates a NEW table `accessDeniedAuditNew` instead of modifying the existing `accessDeniedAudit` table. This violates the build spec which says to modify `lib/db/schema.ts` to add the table. The existing `accessDeniedAudit` table has different columns than specified. The endpoint and migration also reference the new table name, so nothing works together.

Migration creates `access_denied_audit_new` instead of `access_denied_audit`. The REVOKE statement uses placeholder `app_user` instead of the actual application DB role. The build spec says to replace <app_role> with the platform's application DB role as documented in FS-SCHEMA-001.
The endpoint inserts into `accessDeniedAuditNew` table, not `access_denied_audit`. This means the acceptance criterion requiring a row in `access_denied_audit` will fail.
